frameworks.name: partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP)
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian framework over hidden environmental states governing both choice and confidence

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference with incomplete knowledge
frameworks.one_line: observer holds imperfect knowledge of environment parameters when updating beliefs

frameworks.name: sub-optimal inference accounts of confidence
frameworks.one_line: view that confidence-accuracy gaps reflect suboptimality or distinct computations

frameworks.name: post-decision wagering
frameworks.one_line: behavioral readout of subjective confidence used to test the model

core_question: Can a single Bayesian framework explain both perceptual choice and its associated confidence, including their well-known dissociations?
key_assumptions: observer infers hidden environmental states from noisy sensory input
choice and confidence arise from a common POMDP-based inference
the observer's knowledge of environmental statistics is incomplete
manipulations: direction-discrimination task with post-decision wagering in monkeys
simultaneous vs sequential reporting of choice and confidence
key_findings: model explains objective accuracy and predicts subjective confidence
model reproduces the hard-easy effect for confidence
model captures opposing effects of stimulus volatility on confidence and accuracy
model accounts for apparent over-weighting of choice-congruent evidence on confidence
interpretations: confidence-accuracy dissociations need not reflect sub-optimality or separate computations
such dissociations emerge naturally from Bayesian inference under incomplete knowledge
